paragraphs: I'm excited to share this opportunity to invest in Puget Sound Therapeutics.
PST is developing a cryopreservation process that will dramatically expand access to corneal tissue and restore sight to millions who are currently blind due to a lack of access to treatment.

I've known PST's founder, Daniel, since college, where he spent his summers as an eye technician harvesting donor corneas. Over the years, I've spent countless hours with him talking about the unique challenges and potential in the space. I've heard all of his trials and tribulations – from the kafkaesque border complications of getting clinical trial materials into El Salvador to the active hurricane threatening his ongoing trials – and have seen how he handled it all with cool, calm resolve.

Last summer, I invested in PST's pre-seed based on Daniel's strong vision, experience, and leadership. Since then, I've spent a ton of time researching and multiple days in the lab, where I've observed first-hand the validated results of their cell culture and cryopreservation process, and can see a clear path forward through the clinical trial phases to an exit. This seed round will fund the execution of their first clinical trial, a key milestone on this path.

My core mission in life is to support and enable the building of the future. I want to live in a world where we're curing diseases, especially those that take away something as important as sight. This rare combination of exceptional return potential and genuine impact is why I'm doubling down and extending this opportunity to all of you.

I've built this dataroom to give you everything you need to reach the same level of conviction I've come to. If you need anything beyond what's here or want to discuss the investment, please call or text me anytime, and I'd be happy to walk you through it.
